equivalence_argument: No current audited code converts block transaction SPC calls into a single SpcVerificationRequest batch for SpcVerifier.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: The block executor does not aggregate all transaction SPC calls into SpcVerificationRequest values or submit them to one verifier batch.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/evm/evm/src/spc.rs defines the trait request type but does not collect block transactions into requests.
crates/ritual-block-verification/src/lib.rs scans settlement-adjacent SPC calls and directly calls verify_signature_core, not SpcVerifier::verify_batch.

rationale: The new extraction helper iterates over recovered block transactions, pulls `txn.spc_calls()`, maps each `SpcCall` into `SpcVerificationRequest`, and `verify_spc_calls_batch` submits the collected vector to `verify_batch` before evaluating aggregate failures. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: For block transactions exposing SPC calls, the executor must convert each call into an `SpcVerificationRequest` carrying the call input, output, proof, precompile address, transaction hash, and block context, then submit all collected requests to the verifier in one batch.
suggested_falsification_tests: Use a mock verifier that records requests, execute a block with two transactions containing distinct SPC calls, and assert that a single `verify_batch` invocation receives one request per call with matching input, output, proof, precompile address, transaction hash, and block number.
summary: Block SPC calls are not batched into SpcVerifier requests.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Instrument SpcVerifier::verify_batch and execute a block with two SPC-bearing transactions; assert one batch receives both requests. The current audited code has no such call site.
title: SPC calls are batched from block transactions
